task_completion.description: The agent successfully loads the abdominal CT scan into 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible with the axial, sagittal, and coronal views populated with grayscale medical imaging data.

task_completion.description: The agent identifies and delineates the abdominal aorta using segmentation or markup tools.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the axial view, the circular vessel anterior to the spine (aorta) is highlighted with a colored segmentation mask (Segment Editor) or traced with a closed curve (Markups).

task_completion.description: The agent generates and displays the cross-sectional area measurement within the Slicer interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Segment Statistics' table is visible showing a value in the 'Area' column (mm²), or the 'Markups' control panel is visible showing an area measurement for the closed curve.

task_completion.description: The agent calculates the equivalent diameter and determines the clinical classification.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Python console, calculator, or text editor is visible showing the conversion of the measured Area to Diameter (d = 2*sqrt(Area/pi)) and the selection of 'Normal', 'Ectatic', or 'Aneurysmal'.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the measurement file (.mrk.json or segmentation) and the JSON report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Data' dialog is open with 'max_aorta_measurement' and 'max_aorta_report' selected, or terminal/console commands are visible saving these files to '~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/'.

task_completion.description: The agent captures and saves the required screenshot of the measurement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent uses the Screen Capture module or executes code to save 'max_aorta_screenshot.png' to the specified directory.

integrity.description: The agent uses the 3D Slicer GUI tools (Segment Editor, Markups, etc.) to derive the measurement rather than guessing or using external info.
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse cursor interaction with Slicer modules/panels is visible; the measurement is not simply typed into a file without prior tool usage.

integrity.description: The measured structure is actually the aorta.
integrity.visual_evidence: The segmentation or curve is placed on the large circular vessel immediately anterior to the vertebral column, not on the spine, kidneys, or empty space.

integrity.description: The values in the final report match the values shown in the Slicer interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'max_csa_mm2' value in the JSON report corresponds to the area measurement visible in the Segment Statistics or Markups table.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Classification thresholds based on equivalent diameter: Normal < 30mm (Area < 706.8 mm²); Ectatic 30-35mm (Area 706.8 - 962.1 mm²); Aneurysmal > 35mm (Area > 962.1 mm²). The abdominal aorta is the large circular vessel located just anterior to the spine.
